# Figure description: 2_Карта_фактов_M-42-III.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-III/2_Карта_фактов_M-42-III.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **Карта фактического материала** (Map of Factual/Actual Material) — a data-coverage or field-data compilation map at 1:200,000 scale. It is a point-location map showing the spatial distribution of all data collection points (boreholes, geophysical stations, observation points) across the survey area, rather than a geological interpretation map. This type of map is standard in Soviet geological reporting to document the completeness and spatial density of field data.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers sheet **М-42-III** of the standard Soviet topographic nomenclature system (1:200,000 scale). It is a single sheet within the broader M-42 and N-42 regional survey described in Report 36572. The area is in **northern Kazakhstan**, administered under the **Министерство геологии Казахской ССР** (Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR) and produced by the **Центрально-Казахстанское геолого-производственное объединение** (Central Kazakhstan Geological-Production Association).

The map is dated **1984** (visible in upper left as "1984г."). A stamp in the center-right of the map reads **Казахская ССР Республиканский геологический фонд, No. 36572**, confirming this belongs to the same report series referenced in the context. The map was compiled at the **ЦПБ Западно-Тенизское** (Central Production Base, West-Teniz) between 1982 and 1984.

---

## Key Content

The map is densely populated with **hundreds of point-location symbols** distributed across the entire sheet, representing individual data collection stations or observation points from various survey methods. The following features are notable:

- **Two distinct symbol types** are used throughout:
  - **Open circles (О)** — likely representing geophysical observation points (gravity, magnetic, or seismic stations)
  - **Filled/solid dots (•)** — likely representing boreholes or drilling points (скважины), or a second category of geophysical measurements

- **Point labels**: Each point carries an alphanumeric label. A systematic prefix system is visible:
  - Labels beginning with **"О2-"** followed by numbers (e.g., О2-4, О2-16, О2-28, О2-48, О2-75, О2-102, О2-128, О2-165, О2-181, etc.) — these appear to be the open-circle station points
  - Labels beginning with **"ОЭ-"** or similar prefixes in certain sectors
  - Numeric-only labels (e.g., 7766, 7745, 7725, 7726, 7729, 1000, 4680, 4228, 2253, 3252, 7792, 2818, 3501, 3252, 8345, 4800, 8344, 3150, 3254, 3448, 3400, 7838, 7845, 5232, 5253, 6095, 2401, 2589, 2460, 2507, 2935) — likely borehole numbers or seismic point identifiers concentrated in clusters, particularly in the left/western and southeastern portions of the map

- **Line networks**: Several **traverses or profile lines** are drawn connecting clusters of points, particularly visible in the northern, central, and southwestern portions. These are likely **seismic profiles** or **geological traverse routes**, connecting sequential observation stations.

- **Polygonal boundaries**: Irregular **closed and open polygonal outlines** are drawn in several areas, possibly delineating:
  - Survey block boundaries
  - Anomaly zones identified during the survey
  - Structural contour areas requiring closer investigation

- **Point density patterns**: The northern half of the map shows a higher density of open-circle stations arranged in rough grid or traverse patterns, suggesting systematic geophysical profiling. The western margin has clusters of numeric-only points (boreholes). The southeastern quadrant appears somewhat less densely covered.

- **No colour** is used — the map is entirely in black and white, consistent with archival Soviet geological documents.

- **No contour values or isoline gradients** are present — this is strictly a data-location map, not an interpretation map.
